name: Macaroon Cake (Gateau Sec Aux Noisettes)
recipeInstructions:
1. Puree the raspberries in a blender with 35 g (1 1/4 oz) of sugar, then strain the mixture.
2. Prepare macaroon paste by mixing the remaining sugar, ground hazelnuts, egg whites, and lemon rind with a spatula.
3. Roll out the pastry to a thickness of 2-3 mm (1/8 in) and line an 18 cm (7 in) tart tin with a removable base. Prick it all over with fork prongs. Refrigerate if not baking immediately.
4. Preheat the oven to 220°C/425°F.
5. Lightly sprinkle caster sugar over the uncooked pastry, then spread the raspberry puree evenly.
6. Spread the macaroon mixture over the raspberry layer, smoothing the surface with a spatula.
7. Bake the cake for 40 minutes in the pre-heated oven. Reduce heat to 180°C/350°F after 20 minutes if browning too quickly. Remove and let cool slightly.
8. Scatter slivered almonds over the warm cake. Sprinkle icing sugar on top, then place under a hot grill briefly to caramelize lightly. Be careful as this takes only about a minute.
9. Let the cake cool completely before removing from the tin.
